title: Cybersecurity Fundamentals
description: Introduction to cybersecurity covering threat modeling, cryptography, network security, web security, and security policy. Students study symmetric and asymmetric encryption, hash functions, digital signatures, PKI, common attack vectors (SQL injection, XSS, buffer overflow), intrusion detection, access control models, and security risk assessment. Lab exercises involve hands-on penetration testing in controlled environments, cryptographic implementations, and security auditing. Students need either CS201 (data structures for understanding security algorithm implementations) or CS304 (networking knowledge for network security topics) as a prerequisite.
topics: Web security: SQL injection, XSS, CSRF
outcomes: Apply threat modeling to identify system vulnerabilities
Identify and mitigate common web application vulnerabilities
Configure network security tools and firewalls
Perform basic security audits and penetration tests
Develop security policies for organizational compliance
